mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending expectations and trigger negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >65% of instances where the unemployment rate rises above the threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates broad-based domestic demand growth that drives corporate revenue expectations higher, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields negative forward returns in >55% of occurrences or the interaction term loses statistical significance in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production growth reflects strengthening manufacturing capacity utilization that boosts short-term equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: The signal fails to predict positive 5-day returns in >60% of cases during early-cycle phases or loses predictive power when inflation exceeds 3%.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation above a 0.4% monthly threshold forces markets to price in tighter monetary policy and margin compression, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Positive forward returns occur in >50% of months where inflation exceeds the threshold, or the signal predictive accuracy drops below random chance.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market tightening that supports wage growth and consumer demand, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The combined signal produces negative forward returns in >55% of instances or the correlation between the two series breaks down during structural labor shifts.

mechanism_narrative: Higher quarterly real GDP growth rates reflect stronger aggregate economic activity that improves corporate earnings forecasts, leading to positive S&P 500 returns within one week of data release.
failure_condition: The GDP growth signal yields negative 5-day returns in >60% of quarters or shows no statistical edge over a buy-and-hold baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in industrial production exceeding 0.5% month-over-month signals weakening manufacturing output and supply chain stress that depresses equity valuations, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >50% of contraction months or the signal loses predictive power when global trade volumes expand simultaneously.
